what:
OpenFn adaptor for Medic Mobile. The adaptor lets OpenFn workflows read from and write to Medic Mobile (legacy; succeeded by Community Health Toolkit / CHT).

It exposes 3 operations that you can call from a workflow step, listed in the Operations tab. Vendor homepage: https://medic.org.
connect:
Medic supports HTTP Basic auth for API access.

The credential schema asks for server (required), db (required), username (required), and password (required). Auth reference: https://docs.communityhealthtoolkit.org/apps/reference/api/.
when:
Reach for this adaptor when your workflow needs to read or write data in Medic Mobile (legacy; succeeded by Community Health Toolkit / CHT).

Triggers haven't been catalogued yet — drive the workflow from a cron schedule or an upstream webhook in the meantime. Medic doesn't appear to expose webhooks, so plan on polling at a sensible interval.
wire:
A typical wiring pattern is trigger → fetch → transform → write.

Drive the workflow from a cron schedule or an upstream webhook trigger. Then chain operations from this adaptor's Operations tab to fetch or write data in Medic. Pass values between steps through state.data — every operation reads from and writes to job state.

To move data between systems, follow this step with another adaptor (e.g. write the fetched records into a downstream FHIR, DHIS2, or database adaptor).
scale:
When running Medic jobs at volume:

- Medic doesn't surface webhooks, so poll on the longest interval your SLA allows and use a since-cursor to avoid re-reading everything.
- Paginate list and search calls explicitly; never assume a single response is the full result set.
- Make jobs idempotent by keying on stable external IDs so a replay doesn't create duplicates.
- Surface 4xx/5xx responses with the vendor request ID in your logs, and treat 429s as retries with backoff.

Adapter note: Legacy adapter — Medic Mobile rebranded to the Community Health Toolkit (CHT). Use the `cht` adapter for new work; kept here for older job compatibility.
best:
- Store Medic credentials in OpenFn's credential store; never inline keys or tokens in job code.
- Pin @openfn/language-medicmobile to v0.5.29 in package.json so an upstream change doesn't break a production workflow silently.
- Keep each step small — one logical API call per operation makes failures easier to diagnose and retry.
- Add defensive checks before transforming inbound payloads — vendor responses change between versions.
- Log enough context (resource id, run id, vendor request id) so an incident can be traced end-to-end.
